creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.
contentSnippet: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.
contentSnippet: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

title: (No AI) The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Hands with too many fingers typing on a keyboard.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/08/AI_Hands_A_Bernis_02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.

[Broad distaste](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/897900/ai-trust-gap-killer-app-vergecast) around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been _a thing_ in [creative communities](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/864951/human-artistry-campaign-ai-licensing-artists), including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the [broad concept](https://x.com/ffe2ff/status/2070804900871634965) of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called [@heatedrivalryai](https://x.com/heatedrivalryai) promised a seemingly more reliable solution …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/960854/ai-fanfiction-ao3-claude-detector)
contentSnippet:
Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge	

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.
Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a thing in creative communities, including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the broad concept of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called @heatedrivalryai promised a seemingly more reliable solution …
Read the full story at The Verge.

But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire. Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a […]

title: (No AI) Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_A_3800fc-1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

At the event ["The Briefing: AI for Science"](https://www.anthropic.com/events/the-briefing-ai-for-science) earlier this week, Anthropic [announced Claude Science](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/959371/claudes-getting-a-lab-coat), a new "[AI workbench for scientists](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-science-ai-workbench)" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominating the industry with its popular coding tools and powerful AI models, framed the launch around what it says is AI's potential to "dramatically accelerate the pace of scientific discovery and the development of healthcare interventions," and touted a long list of biotech and pharma customers already using Claude.

Anthropic also went a step further, saying it would develop drugs of its …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/961311/anthropic-claude-science-ai-drug-development)

At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic also went a step further, saying it would develop drugs of its  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
content: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.
contentSnippet: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

title: (No AI) Core dump epidemiology: fixing an 18-year-old bug
content: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.
contentSnippet: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.

title: (No AI) Google DeepMind Unionization Talks Are Off to a Rocky Start
dc:creator: Joel Khalili
content: During negotiations on Wednesday, employees voiced frustrations with what they consider an unwillingness among executives to engage meaningfully with the prospect of unionization.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Can Cursor Remain a Platform for OpenAI and Anthropic’s Models Inside SpaceX?
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
contentSnippet: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.
